members.name: Jetsunma Tenzin Palmo
members.card: Bhikṣuṇī, Drukpa Kagyu lineage. Founder of Dongyu Gatsal Ling Nunnery.
members.bio: Jetsunma Tenzin Palmo (born 1943) is a bhikṣuṇī in the Drukpa Lineage of the Kagyu school of Tibetan Buddhism. She is an author, teacher, and founder of the Dongyu Gatsal Ling Nunnery in Himachal Pradesh, India. She is best known for having spent twelve years living in a remote cave in the Himalayas, three of those years in strict meditation retreat.

members.name: Eun-su Cho
members.card: Professor of Buddhist philosophy, Seoul National University.
members.bio: Eun-su Cho is a professor of Buddhist philosophy at Seoul National University and a longtime scholar of Korean Buddhism and Buddhist women's history. She served on the Sakyadhita Executive Committee during the 2013–2021 term.

members.name: Kaytee Sumida
members.card: Lifetime member; liaison, Interreligious Council of San Diego.
members.bio: Kaytee Sumida is a lifetime member of Sakyadhita who has served on the Executive Committee across multiple terms. She has been a member of the Buddhist Temple San Diego since 1990 and serves as liaison with the Interreligious Council of San Diego.

members.name: Lien Bui
members.card: Served on the Sakyadhita Executive Committee.
members.bio: Lien Bui served as Treasurer on the Sakyadhita Executive Committee during the 2013–2021 term.
